paragraphs.text: Consumers are deceived in many ways, traders tell you a game, but then you only give you a licence to play. When a consumer buys a video game, he expects to continue to play it in the future, even if it has online functionalities. Producers of video games should be obliged to make games offline if the operator no longer wants to offer the online features. It is also possible for the stores of the large consoles manufacturers to buy in for another EU country only under significant restrictions. Either it is not possible to separate credit balances and my purchases by store, or the store is only available in the national language. Unfortunately, I am not mastering all 24 EU languages. There is no cross-border shopping and therefore a common market. I want to be able to compare prices in all the stores of these manufacturers for the EU Member States in order to find the best offer, and I want to do so in my national language and without restrictions on credit and access to these games. The large consoles manufacturers use their closed consoles system to link essential functions, such as online games, to a subscription and thus create a dependency. This abo for online games is required even if the game uses peer-to-peer technology and therefore does not impose any burden on someone other than the consumer when playing online. Basic features such as online games should be offered free of charge. Especially in the area of mobile gaming, so-called Gacha mechanisms exist, you pay a sum and can then get something valuable or rather worthless. The principle is the same as for other games of chance. You can, of course, sell an account with valuable items on other online platforms by sharing username and password. However, as these Gacha mechanisms are also included in games for young people, there is a potential for addiction and games of chance by minors. Mechanisms similar to gambling and gambling should be prohibited in video games for minors when they require real money. I hope that there is a need for action in the gaming field.
